name: prescriptions_sample
description: Sample prescription dataset for clinical validation

documents.document_text: Patient: John Miller, DOB: 05/12/1978. Prescription: Metformin Hydrochloride 500 mg extended-release tablets. Dispense #60. Sig: Take one tablet by mouth once daily with the evening meal. Refills: 3. Diagnosis: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (E11.9). Prescriber: Dr. Sarah Chen, MD.

entries.question: What is the dosage and frequency of Metformin prescribed?
entries.expected_answer: Metformin Hydrochloride 500 mg extended-release tablets, one tablet once daily with the evening meal. Dispensed 60 tablets with 3 refills.

entries.question: What diagnosis is being treated with this prescription?
entries.expected_answer: The patient is being treated for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (ICD-10 code E11.9).

entries.question: How many refills are authorized and what is the total duration of therapy?
entries.expected_answer: Three refills are authorized. With a 30-day supply per fill (60 tablets at 1 per day), the total therapy covers approximately 120 days or 4 months, assuming monthly dispensing.

documents.document_text: Patient: Susan Walker, DOB: 11/03/1965. Prescription: Atorvastatin Calcium 40 mg tablets. Dispense #30. Sig: Take one tablet by mouth daily at bedtime. Refills: 5. Diagnosis: Hyperlipidemia, Mixed (E78.2). Also noted: patient has history of coronary artery disease. LDL target <70 mg/dL. Prescriber: Dr. James Park, MD.

structured_extraction.notes: History of coronary artery disease. LDL target <70 mg/dL.

entries.question: What is the prescribed statin therapy and its indication?
entries.expected_answer: Atorvastatin Calcium 40 mg daily at bedtime for Mixed Hyperlipidemia (E78.2) in a patient with a history of coronary artery disease. The LDL target is less than 70 mg/dL, consistent with secondary prevention guidelines.

entries.question: Why was a 40 mg dose of Atorvastatin selected instead of a lower dose?
entries.expected_answer: The 40 mg moderate-to-high intensity dose is appropriate given the patient has established coronary artery disease (secondary prevention) and a LDL target below 70 mg/dL. High-intensity statin therapy (Atorvastatin 40-80 mg) is guideline-recommended for patients with ASCVD.

documents.document_text: Patient: Emily Torres, DOB: 08/22/1995. Prescription: Amoxicillin 500 mg capsules. Dispense #21. Sig: Take one capsule by mouth three times daily for 7 days. Refills: 0. Diagnosis: Acute bacterial sinusitis (J01.90). Allergies: None reported. Prescriber: Dr. Lisa Nguyen, DO.

entries.question: What antibiotic is prescribed and for what duration?
entries.expected_answer: Amoxicillin 500 mg, one capsule three times daily for 7 days (21 capsules total). No refills. Diagnosis: acute bacterial sinusitis.

entries.question: Is the antibiotic selection appropriate for acute bacterial sinusitis?
entries.expected_answer: Yes, Amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily for 5-7 days is a first-line empiric therapy for acute bacterial sinusitis per IDSA guidelines. The patient has no reported allergies. The 7-day duration is appropriate. The no-refill policy is correct for antibiotic courses to complete the full treatment.
